Torii and haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}Takatsuki-chō Takatsuki | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}
{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|WD=Q21995144|lt=Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}
Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era (729-749) | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=ikken-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503|1=April 8}} | 神事 = {{ill|lt=Okonai|Q11291908|WD=Q11291908|1=Okonai}} ({{ill|lt=February 8|Q2323|WD=Q2323|1=February 8}}) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kamitakatsuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Kamitakatsuki Shrine''' (かみたかつきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Takatsuki, Takatsuki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. ===Enshrined deities=== * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|WD=Q21995144|lt=Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} ===Shrine crest=== * Left-dancing Mitsu-gashiwa ===Shrine name=== There is a tradition that {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} descended upon a giant {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|lt=tsuki tree|1=Zelkova serrata}} at Kurihara, Aza Unenogahara (Une, Takatsuki-chō), and a shrine was built in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era (729-749) to worship Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto.
Later, a shrine building enshrining Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto together was said to have been founded by {{ill|lt=Heguri Hirowaza|Q11483498|WD=Q11483498|lt=Heguri Hirowaza|1=Heguri Clan}}.
In the {{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} era (1087-1094), when {{ill|lt=Ōe no Masafusa|Q3135978|WD=Q3135978|1=Ōe no Masafusa}} visited this area and praised the moon with a waka, it is said "Takatsuki" was later changed to "Takatsuki (高月)."
It was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of seven villages - Une, Ahe, Takatsuki, Morimoto, Ochikawa, Mōjō, and Doganji (roughly the former {{ill|lt=Minami-Tominaga Village|Q17175516|WD=Q17175516|1=Minamitominaga}} area).
In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.[https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1219 Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association] Until {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, neighboring {{ill|lt=Daien-ji (Nagahama)|UNKNOWN|lt=Daien-ji}} served as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=ikken-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} *Haiden: {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} *{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} *{{ill|lt=Tenman-gū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} *Ryūjin Shrine ===Festivals / annual events=== * {{ill|lt=Okonai|Q11291908|WD=Q11291908|1=Okonai}} - {{ill|lt=February 8|Q2323|WD=Q2323|1=February 8}} * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} - {{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} ===Location / transportation=== {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}Takatsuki-chō Takatsuki * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q5365710|WD=Q5365710|1=Takatsuki Station (Shiga)}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|Q1318747|WD=Q1318747|1=Hokuriku Main Line}} ===Notes / references=== {{脚注ヘルプ}} ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Takatsuki-chō|Q1042574|WD=Q1042574|1=Takatsuki, Shiga}} *{{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q5365710|WD=Q5365710|1=Takatsuki Station (Shiga)}} *{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|WD=Q21995144|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefectural Route 279 Ochikawa-Takatsuki Line|Q11564833|WD=Q11564833|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 279}} *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefectural Route 280 Iguchi-Takatsuki Line|Q11564837|WD=Q11564837|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 280}} ===External links=== *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1219 Kamitakatsuki Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamitakatsuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamitakatsuki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:長浜市の建築物]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era (729-749) | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=ikken-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503|1=April 8}} | 神事 = {{ill|lt=Okonai|Q11291908|WD=Q11291908|1=Okonai}} ({{ill|lt=February 8|Q2323|WD=Q2323|1=February 8}}) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kamitakatsuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Kamitakatsuki Shrine''' (かみたかつきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Takatsuki, Takatsuki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. ===Enshrined deities=== * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|WD=Q21995144|lt=Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} ===Shrine crest=== * Left-dancing Mitsu-gashiwa ===Shrine name=== There is a tradition that {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} descended upon a giant {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|lt=tsuki tree|1=Zelkova serrata}} at Kurihara, Aza Unenogahara (Une, Takatsuki-chō), and a shrine was built in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era (729-749) to worship Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto.
Later, a shrine building enshrining Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto together was said to have been founded by {{ill|lt=Heguri Hirowaza|Q11483498|WD=Q11483498|lt=Heguri Hirowaza|1=Heguri Clan}}.
In the {{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} era (1087-1094), when {{ill|lt=Ōe no Masafusa|Q3135978|WD=Q3135978|1=Ōe no Masafusa}} visited this area and praised the moon with a waka, it is said "Takatsuki" was later changed to "Takatsuki (高月)."
It was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of seven villages - Une, Ahe, Takatsuki, Morimoto, Ochikawa, Mōjō, and Doganji (roughly the former {{ill|lt=Minami-Tominaga Village|Q17175516|WD=Q17175516|1=Minamitominaga}} area).
In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.[https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1219 Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association] Until {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, neighboring {{ill|lt=Daien-ji (Nagahama)|UNKNOWN|lt=Daien-ji}} served as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=ikken-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} *Haiden: {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} *{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} *{{ill|lt=Tenman-gū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} *Ryūjin Shrine ===Festivals / annual events=== * {{ill|lt=Okonai|Q11291908|WD=Q11291908|1=Okonai}} - {{ill|lt=February 8|Q2323|WD=Q2323|1=February 8}} * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} - {{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} ===Location / transportation=== {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}Takatsuki-chō Takatsuki * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q5365710|WD=Q5365710|1=Takatsuki Station (Shiga)}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|Q1318747|WD=Q1318747|1=Hokuriku Main Line}} ===Notes / references=== {{脚注ヘルプ}} ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Takatsuki-chō|Q1042574|WD=Q1042574|1=Takatsuki, Shiga}} *{{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q5365710|WD=Q5365710|1=Takatsuki Station (Shiga)}} *{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|WD=Q21995144|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefectural Route 279 Ochikawa-Takatsuki Line|Q11564833|WD=Q11564833|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 279}} *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefectural Route 280 Iguchi-Takatsuki Line|Q11564837|WD=Q11564837|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 280}} ===External links=== *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1219 Kamitakatsuki Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamitakatsuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamitakatsuki Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Torii and haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}Takatsuki-chō Takatsuki | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}
{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|WD=Q21995144|lt=Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}}
Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era (729-749) | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=ikken-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503|1=April 8}} | 神事 = {{ill|lt=Okonai|Q11291908|WD=Q11291908|1=Okonai}} ({{ill|lt=February 8|Q2323|WD=Q2323|1=February 8}}) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kamitakatsuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Kamitakatsuki Shrine''' (かみたかつきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Takatsuki, Takatsuki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. ===Enshrined deities=== * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|WD=Q21995144|lt=Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} ===Shrine crest=== * Left-dancing Mitsu-gashiwa ===Shrine name=== There is a tradition that {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} descended upon a giant {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|lt=tsuki tree|1=Zelkova serrata}} at Kurihara, Aza Unenogahara (Une, Takatsuki-chō), and a shrine was built in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era (729-749) to worship Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto.
Later, a shrine building enshrining Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto together was said to have been founded by {{ill|lt=Heguri Hirowaza|Q11483498|WD=Q11483498|lt=Heguri Hirowaza|1=Heguri Clan}}.
In the {{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} era (1087-1094), when {{ill|lt=Ōe no Masafusa|Q3135978|WD=Q3135978|1=Ōe no Masafusa}} visited this area and praised the moon with a waka, it is said "Takatsuki" was later changed to "Takatsuki (高月)."
It was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of seven villages - Une, Ahe, Takatsuki, Morimoto, Ochikawa, Mōjō, and Doganji (roughly the former {{ill|lt=Minami-Tominaga Village|Q17175516|WD=Q17175516|1=Minamitominaga}} area).
In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.[https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1219 Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association] Until {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, neighboring {{ill|lt=Daien-ji (Nagahama)|UNKNOWN|lt=Daien-ji}} served as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=ikken-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} *Haiden: {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} *{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} *{{ill|lt=Tenman-gū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} *Ryūjin Shrine ===Festivals / annual events=== * {{ill|lt=Okonai|Q11291908|WD=Q11291908|1=Okonai}} - {{ill|lt=February 8|Q2323|WD=Q2323|1=February 8}} * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} - {{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} ===Location / transportation=== {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}Takatsuki-chō Takatsuki * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q5365710|WD=Q5365710|1=Takatsuki Station (Shiga)}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|Q1318747|WD=Q1318747|1=Hokuriku Main Line}} ===Notes / references=== {{脚注ヘルプ}} ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Takatsuki-chō|Q1042574|WD=Q1042574|1=Takatsuki, Shiga}} *{{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q5365710|WD=Q5365710|1=Takatsuki Station (Shiga)}} *{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|WD=Q21995144|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefectural Route 279 Ochikawa-Takatsuki Line|Q11564833|WD=Q11564833|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 279}} *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefectural Route 280 Iguchi-Takatsuki Line|Q11564837|WD=Q11564837|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 280}} ===External links=== *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1219 Kamitakatsuki Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamitakatsuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamitakatsuki Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era (729-749) | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=ikken-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|WD=Q2503|1=April 8}} | 神事 = {{ill|lt=Okonai|Q11291908|WD=Q11291908|1=Okonai}} ({{ill|lt=February 8|Q2323|WD=Q2323|1=February 8}}) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kamitakatsuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Kamitakatsuki Shrine''' (かみたかつきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Takatsuki, Takatsuki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. ===Enshrined deities=== * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|WD=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|WD=Q21995144|lt=Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} ===Shrine crest=== * Left-dancing Mitsu-gashiwa ===Shrine name=== There is a tradition that {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} descended upon a giant {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|WD=Q920344|lt=tsuki tree|1=Zelkova serrata}} at Kurihara, Aza Unenogahara (Une, Takatsuki-chō), and a shrine was built in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|WD=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era (729-749) to worship Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto.
Later, a shrine building enshrining Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto together was said to have been founded by {{ill|lt=Heguri Hirowaza|Q11483498|WD=Q11483498|lt=Heguri Hirowaza|1=Heguri Clan}}.
In the {{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|WD=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} era (1087-1094), when {{ill|lt=Ōe no Masafusa|Q3135978|WD=Q3135978|1=Ōe no Masafusa}} visited this area and praised the moon with a waka, it is said "Takatsuki" was later changed to "Takatsuki (高月)."
It was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of seven villages - Une, Ahe, Takatsuki, Morimoto, Ochikawa, Mōjō, and Doganji (roughly the former {{ill|lt=Minami-Tominaga Village|Q17175516|WD=Q17175516|1=Minamitominaga}} area).
In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|WD=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.[https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1219 Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association] Until {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, neighboring {{ill|lt=Daien-ji (Nagahama)|UNKNOWN|lt=Daien-ji}} served as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|lt=ikken-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} *Haiden: {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} *{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} *{{ill|lt=Tenman-gū|Q798838|WD=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} *Ryūjin Shrine ===Festivals / annual events=== * {{ill|lt=Okonai|Q11291908|WD=Q11291908|1=Okonai}} - {{ill|lt=February 8|Q2323|WD=Q2323|1=February 8}} * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} - {{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|WD=Q2524|1=April 20}} ===Location / transportation=== {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|WD=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}Takatsuki-chō Takatsuki * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q5365710|WD=Q5365710|1=Takatsuki Station (Shiga)}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|Q1318747|WD=Q1318747|1=Hokuriku Main Line}} ===Notes / references=== {{脚注ヘルプ}} ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Takatsuki-chō|Q1042574|WD=Q1042574|1=Takatsuki, Shiga}} *{{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q5365710|WD=Q5365710|1=Takatsuki Station (Shiga)}} *{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|WD=Q21995144|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefectural Route 279 Ochikawa-Takatsuki Line|Q11564833|WD=Q11564833|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 279}} *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefectural Route 280 Iguchi-Takatsuki Line|Q11564837|WD=Q11564837|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 280}} ===External links=== *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1219 Kamitakatsuki Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamitakatsuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamitakatsuki Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|qid=Q21995144|lt=Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}}
Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto | 神体 = | 社格 = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era (729-749) | 本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=ikken-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = {{ill|lt=April 8|Q2503|qid=Q2503|1=April 8}} | 神事 = {{ill|lt=Okonai|Q11291908|qid=Q11291908|1=Okonai}} ({{ill|lt=February 8|Q2323|qid=Q2323|1=February 8}}) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Kamitakatsuki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} '''Kamitakatsuki Shrine''' (かみたかつきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Takatsuki, Takatsuki-chō, {{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|qid=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} recorded in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. ===Enshrined deities=== * {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} * {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|qid=Q21995144|lt=Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} ===Shrine crest=== * Left-dancing Mitsu-gashiwa ===Shrine name=== There is a tradition that {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} descended upon a giant {{ill|lt=zelkova|Q920344|qid=Q920344|lt=tsuki tree|1=Zelkova serrata}} at Kurihara, Aza Unenogahara (Une, Takatsuki-chō), and a shrine was built in the {{ill|lt=Tenpyō|Q1199879|qid=Q1199879|1=Tenpyō}} era (729-749) to worship Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto.
Later, a shrine building enshrining Takanuka-hime-no-Mikoto together was said to have been founded by {{ill|lt=Heguri Hirowaza|Q11483498|qid=Q11483498|lt=Heguri Hirowaza|1=Heguri Clan}}.
In the {{ill|lt=Kanji|Q1193874|qid=Q1193874|1=Kanji (era)}} era (1087-1094), when {{ill|lt=Ōe no Masafusa|Q3135978|qid=Q3135978|1=Ōe no Masafusa}} visited this area and praised the moon with a waka, it is said "Takatsuki" was later changed to "Takatsuki (高月)."
It was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of seven villages - Une, Ahe, Takatsuki, Morimoto, Ochikawa, Mōjō, and Doganji (roughly the former {{ill|lt=Minami-Tominaga Village|Q17175516|qid=Q17175516|1=Minamitominaga}} area).
In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|qid=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.[https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1219 Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association] Until {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, neighboring {{ill|lt=Daien-ji (Nagahama)|UNKNOWN|lt=Daien-ji}} served as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=ikken-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} *Haiden: {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} *{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|qid=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} *{{ill|lt=Tenman-gū|Q798838|qid=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} *Ryūjin Shrine ===Festivals / annual events=== * {{ill|lt=Okonai|Q11291908|qid=Q11291908|1=Okonai}} - {{ill|lt=February 8|Q2323|qid=Q2323|1=February 8}} * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} - {{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|qid=Q2524|1=April 20}} ===Location / transportation=== {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|qid=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}Takatsuki-chō Takatsuki * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q5365710|qid=Q5365710|1=Takatsuki Station (Shiga)}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|Q1318747|qid=Q1318747|1=Hokuriku Main Line}} ===Notes / references=== {{脚注ヘルプ}} ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Takatsuki-chō|Q1042574|qid=Q1042574|1=Takatsuki, Shiga}} *{{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q5365710|qid=Q5365710|1=Takatsuki Station (Shiga)}} *{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|qid=Q21995144|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefectural Route 279 Ochikawa-Takatsuki Line|Q11564833|qid=Q11564833|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 279}} *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefectural Route 280 Iguchi-Takatsuki Line|Q11564837|qid=Q11564837|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 280}} ===External links=== *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1219 Kamitakatsuki Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamitakatsuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamitakatsuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]

It was the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=sōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} of seven villages - Une, Ahe, Takatsuki, Morimoto, Ochikawa, Mōjō, and Doganji (roughly the former {{ill|lt=Minami-Tominaga Village|Q17175516|qid=Q17175516|1=Minamitominaga}} area).
In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1876|Q7766|qid=Q7766|1=1876}}), it was ranked {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}.[https://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1219 Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association] Until {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, neighboring {{ill|lt=Daien-ji (Nagahama)|UNKNOWN|lt=Daien-ji}} served as its {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} temple. ===Shrine buildings / precinct=== *Honden: {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|lt=ikken-sha nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} *Haiden: {{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} *{{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|qid=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} *{{ill|lt=Tenman-gū|Q798838|qid=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} *Ryūjin Shrine ===Festivals / annual events=== * {{ill|lt=Okonai|Q11291908|qid=Q11291908|1=Okonai}} - {{ill|lt=February 8|Q2323|qid=Q2323|1=February 8}} * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|qid=Q11385469|1=Reisai}} - {{ill|lt=April 20|Q2524|qid=Q2524|1=April 20}} ===Location / transportation=== {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagahama|Q844130|qid=Q844130|1=Nagahama, Shiga}}Takatsuki-chō Takatsuki * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q5365710|qid=Q5365710|1=Takatsuki Station (Shiga)}} on {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|qid=Q502125|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Hokuriku Main Line|Q1318747|qid=Q1318747|1=Hokuriku Main Line}} ===Notes / references=== {{脚注ヘルプ}} ===See also=== *{{ill|lt=Takatsuki-chō|Q1042574|qid=Q1042574|1=Takatsuki, Shiga}} *{{ill|lt=Takatsuki Station|Q5365710|qid=Q5365710|1=Takatsuki Station (Shiga)}} *{{ill|lt=Katsuragi Takanuka-hime|Q21995144|qid=Q21995144|1=Kazuraki no Takanukahime}} *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefectural Route 279 Ochikawa-Takatsuki Line|Q11564833|qid=Q11564833|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 279}} *{{ill|lt=Shiga Prefectural Route 280 Iguchi-Takatsuki Line|Q11564837|qid=Q11564837|1=Shiga Prefectural Road Route 280}} ===External links=== *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1219 Kamitakatsuki Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) {{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamitakatsuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamitakatsuki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:長浜市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nagahama, Shiga]] [[Category:近江国の式内社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧村社]]
